title: Masimo Rad-97 Pulse Oximeter - Sensor / SpO₂ Reading Errors
description: Guide for troubleshooting inaccurate SpO₂ readings and sensor errors on the Masimo Rad-97 pulse oximeter, emphasizing external causes first.

steps.title: Check Sensor Connection
steps.instructions: Verify the sensor is fully seated in the oximeter port. Inspect the cable for visible damage, kinks, or loose connectors. Why: Loose or damaged connections can produce intermittent or false readings.

steps.title: Inspect Sensor Condition
steps.instructions: Look for wear, frayed wires, or discoloration on reusable sensors. Ensure disposable sensors are not expired. Why: Damaged or expired sensors often fail to read accurately.

steps.title: Verify Proper Sensor Placement on Patient
steps.instructions: Ensure the sensor is applied to a suitable site (finger, toe, or earlobe). Remove nail polish, artificial nails, or excessive moisture if present. Why: Poor placement or optical interference can cause erroneous SpO₂ values.

steps.title: Check for Patient Movement or Perfusion Issues
steps.instructions: Ask the patient to remain still; check for cold extremities or poor perfusion. Use a different site if needed. Why: Motion artifacts or low perfusion can trigger sensor errors or unstable readings.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the Rad-97 off, wait 10 seconds, then power it back on. Why: Sometimes software resets can clear temporary sensor communication issues.

steps.title: Swap Cables or Sensors if Available
steps.instructions: If another compatible sensor or cable is available, connect it and test. Why: Confirms whether the issue is with the sensor or the oximeter itself.

CCR.Complaint: Rad-97 showing fluctuating SpO₂ readings and sensor error alarms.
CCR.Cause: Sensor improperly seated; cable checked and no visible damage; readings unstable despite repositioning.
CCR.Resolution: Sensor reseated; attempted alternative site and sensor; issue persists—device removed from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual readings or alarms displayed

title: Masimo Rad-97 Pulse Oximeter - Touchscreen / Control Panel Problems
description: Guide for troubleshooting touchscreen or control panel issues on the Masimo Rad-97 pulse oximeter.

steps.title: Ensure Device is Powered Correctly
steps.instructions: Confirm the device battery is charged or the power adapter is connected securely. A low battery or intermittent power can cause touchscreen malfunction.

steps.title: Inspect Screen for Debris or Damage
steps.instructions: Clean the touchscreen with a soft, lint-free cloth and approved disinfectant. Check for cracks, scratches, or residue that might interfere with touch sensitivity.

steps.title: Check User Gloves / Protective Covers
steps.instructions: Some medical gloves or screen protectors reduce touchscreen responsiveness. Remove any covers and test touch response.

steps.title: Restart the Device
steps.instructions: Perform a proper power cycle: turn off, wait 10–15 seconds, and turn back on. This can clear temporary software glitches affecting the touchscreen.

steps.title: Verify Software / Settings
steps.instructions: Check that the device is running the latest approved firmware version. Reset display settings to default if the touchscreen remains unresponsive.

steps.title: Inspect External Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure any wired probes or modules are securely attached. Loose connections can sometimes interfere with system responsiveness.

steps.title: Test Basic Input Functions
steps.instructions: Press physical buttons (if available) to confirm that non-touch input works. If physical buttons work but touch does not, the issue is likely localized to the touchscreen.

CCR.Complaint: User reported the Rad-97 touchscreen was unresponsive during patient monitoring.
CCR.Cause: Observed that the touchscreen did not respond to touch inputs; device powered normally and cables were secure; screen was clean and undamaged.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair. Patient monitored on alternate device during troubleshooting.

helpfulDetails: Battery level at time of failure
Response of physical buttons if present
Presence of gloves or protective screen covers

title: Masimo Rad-97 Pulse Oximeter Pulse Oximeter - Power / Battery Issues
description: Guide for troubleshooting power and battery issues on the Masimo Rad-97 pulse oximeter, focusing on external causes first.

steps.title: Verify Battery Charge and Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure the device is connected to the approved charger. Check that the charging contacts on both the battery and device are clean, free of debris, and aligned. Observe the charging indicator LED: solid or blinking light per Masimo specifications indicates charging status.

steps.title: Test With a Known Good Power Source
steps.instructions: Plug the charger into a verified working outlet. Avoid power strips with switches or surge protectors that might interrupt current.

steps.title: Remove and Reinstall the Battery
steps.instructions: Power down the device if possible. Remove the battery, inspect for corrosion, swelling, or damage. Reinsert securely and attempt to power on.

steps.title: Try a Known Good Battery
steps.instructions: If available, replace the battery with a fully charged, verified working battery. Power on the device.

steps.title: Check for Firmware or Device Alerts
steps.instructions: Some Rad-97 units may display battery or power warnings in the interface. Note any codes or messages for documentation and potential escalation.

CCR.Complaint: Rad-97 would not power on; patient monitoring interrupted.
CCR.Cause: Battery failed to hold charge; charger and power source verified.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent to Biomedical Engineering for battery replacement and functional testing.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual noises, smells, or heat from battery
Device behavior when using a replacement battery

title: Masimo Rad-5 Pulse Oximeter - Sensor Not Detected or Low Perfusion Errors
description: Troubleshooting sensor detection failures and low perfusion errors on the Masimo Rad-5 pulse oximeter.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from active patient monitoring if readings are unreliable and transfer monitoring to a known working pulse oximeter.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Power on the device and check for sensor not detected messages, low perfusion indicators, or intermittent readings.

steps.title: Check Sensor Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the connector is fully seated, reconnect firmly, and inspect for bent pins or debris.

steps.title: Inspect Sensor and Cable Condition
steps.instructions: Look for cracked housing, frayed wires, or dirty sensor surfaces and clean if needed.

steps.title: Try a Known Good Sensor
steps.instructions: Swap with a verified working sensor to isolate whether the issue is sensor-related.

steps.title: Check Patient Application and Site
steps.instructions: Ensure proper placement, remove nail polish, warm extremities, and try alternate sites.

steps.title: Assess Environmental and Motion Factors
steps.instructions: Reduce patient movement and shield from bright ambient light.

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset
steps.instructions: Turn the device off, wait 10–15 seconds, and power back on.

CCR.Complaint: Pulse oximeter not detecting sensor and showing low perfusion error.
CCR.Cause: Damaged sensor cable causing intermittent signal loss.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced sensor with known good unit and verified proper operation.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (working or removed from service)

title: Masimo Rad-5 Pulse Oximeter - SpO2 Readings Inaccurate or Unstable
description: Troubleshooting inaccurate or unstable SpO2 readings caused by sensor issues, patient factors, or environmental interference.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If readings are unreliable, do not depend on this device for clinical decisions. Move the patient to a known working monitor.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Observe the device while in use. Check if readings fluctuate rapidly, compare SpO2 with another device, and note alarms or low perfusion indicators.

steps.title: Check Sensor Placement and Condition (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Inspect and reposition the sensor. Ensure proper placement, remove nail polish, confirm alignment, and inspect for damage.

steps.title: Assess Patient Conditions (Perfusion and Motion)
steps.instructions: Check for poor circulation, cold extremities, or excessive movement. Try a different monitoring site if needed.

steps.title: Check Sensor Cable and Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the sensor cable is fully seated, inspect for bent pins, and swap with a known working sensor if available.

steps.title: Evaluate Ambient Light Interference
steps.instructions: Shield the sensor from bright light and ensure proper closure and fit.

steps.title: Check Device Settings and Mode
steps.instructions: Confirm correct patient mode and ensure no incorrect averaging or sensitivity settings are applied.

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset
steps.instructions: Turn off the unit, remove and reinsert batteries, and power the device back on.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported unstable and fluctuating SpO2 readings on Masimo Rad-5.
CCR.Cause: Damaged and misaligned finger sensor causing poor signal detection.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced sensor with known good unit; verified stable and accurate readings.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Masimo Rad-5 Pulse Oximeter - Battery Not Charging or Short Runtime
description: Battery not charging or short runtime due to adapter, battery, or connection issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from active patient use if battery reliability is in question and use a known working unit instead.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Power on the device and observe for battery indicator not increasing, rapid drain, or unexpected shutdown.

steps.title: Check Power Adapter and Outlet
steps.instructions: Ensure adapter is firmly connected, test outlet with another device, and inspect adapter for damage.

steps.title: Inspect Charging Port and Connections
steps.instructions: Check for debris, bent pins, or looseness and reseat the connector.

steps.title: Check for Charging Indicator Response
steps.instructions: Look for battery icon or LED response and check for intermittent charging by gently moving the connector.

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset
steps.instructions: Turn off device, disconnect power, reseat battery if applicable, reconnect, and restart.

steps.title: Evaluate Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Determine if battery drains quickly, only works plugged in, or fails to hold charge.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Components
steps.instructions: Swap with known good adapter and battery to isolate the faulty component.

CCR.Complaint: Device not charging and shuts off shortly after unplugging.
CCR.Cause: Failed internal battery unable to hold charge after testing with known good adapter.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for battery replacement and evaluation.

title: Masimo Rad-5 Pulse Oximeter - Display or Button Failure
description: Troubleshooting unresponsive buttons, blank or dim display, or intermittent screen operation on the Masimo Rad-5 pulse oximeter.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from active patient use if the display or controls are not functioning reliably. Use an alternate monitoring device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Power on the device and confirm the failure. Observe for completely blank screen, dim or flickering display, or buttons not responding or delayed response.

steps.title: Check Battery Status and Power Source
steps.instructions: Replace batteries with new, known-good batteries, ensure correct battery orientation, and power cycle the device.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Examine the screen and keypad area for cracked display, worn or stuck buttons, fluid ingress or residue, and dirt or debris around buttons.

steps.title: Perform a Hard Reset (Power Cycle)
steps.instructions: Remove batteries, wait 30–60 seconds, reinstall batteries, and power on the device.

steps.title: Check Display Visibility Settings and Environment
steps.instructions: Adjust viewing angle, move device to better lighting, and check for faint display indicating backlight failure.

steps.title: Test Button Functionality Individually
steps.instructions: Press each button and observe response for no response, intermittent response, or only certain buttons failing.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported Rad-5 display blank and buttons not responding during spot check.
CCR.Cause: Depleted batteries caused device to power intermittently, affecting display and keypad function.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced batteries, verified normal display and button operation, returned device to service.

helpfulDetails: Presence of physical damage or contamination

title: Masimo Rad-5 Pulse Oximeter - Alarm or Audio Failure
description: Troubleshooting loss of audible alarms or audio issues on the Masimo Rad-5 pulse oximeter.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If alarms are not audible, remove the device from active patient monitoring and transfer the patient to a functioning monitor.

steps.title: Verify Alarm and Audio Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device and check for no sound during alarms, no keypress tones, low or distorted audio, or intermittent sound.

steps.title: Check Volume Settings (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Access the settings menu, ensure alarm volume is turned up, confirm the device is not muted, and cycle through volume levels.

steps.title: Verify Alarm Limits Are Active
steps.instructions: Ensure SpO2 and pulse rate limits are enabled and trigger a test alarm if safe.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Power off the device, remove and reseat batteries if applicable, and restart the device.

steps.title: Inspect Speaker Area
steps.instructions: Inspect the speaker grille for debris, ensure nothing is covering it, and listen for faint output.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage or Liquid Exposure
steps.instructions: Inspect the housing for cracks, impact damage, or fluid ingress and note any corrosion or unusual smells.

steps.title: Test with Known Alarm Condition
steps.instructions: If safe, simulate a low SpO2 or disconnect the sensor briefly to confirm audible alarm function.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported no audible alarms on Rad-5 during patient monitoring.
CCR.Cause: Internal speaker failure confirmed after volume and alarm settings verified functional.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and sent to vendor for repair.

helpfulDetails: Any visible damage or fluid exposure
